content.full_text:
ART BLOOMER
4 Pintail
Irvine, CA 92714

JUL 20 2:11M. '90

July 20, 1990

Mr. Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Rothman:

I am writing to request your opinion on a possible conflict of interest that I may have as a City Council Member for the City of Irvine.

Currently before the City Council is a matter involving the McDonnell Douglas Realty Company and a Development Agreement for construction of a high-rise office building and monorail on McDonnell Douglas property known as Douglas Plaza. The following are facts concerning my situation:

(1) I am a Senior Vice-President of American Protective Services, Inc., an industrial security company. The company is headquartered in Oakland and is a privately held Sub Chapter S Corporation with 9 shareholders, of which I am one. I am the 5th principal officer of the company which has 11,000 employees and annual sales of approximately $180 million. My ownership consists of 380 shares of restricted common stock which is less than 1/4 of 1% of the entire company. In addition to my salary I also share in profits, dividends and twice yearly bonuses tied to overall company performance and not to individual contracts.

(2) My company, APS, Inc., has a contract to provide security guard services to McDonnell Douglas Realty at the Douglas Plaza. Annual revenues from this contract are approximately $135,000. Approval of this project could possibly result in additional revenues to my company from McDonnell Douglas because of the potential requirement for added security guard coverage.

(3) In addition to the above my company also provides security services to McDonnell Douglas Aerospace Information Systems Company in Santa Ana and Cypress.

(4) In addition to McDonnell Douglas, I currently provide security services for the Birtcher Corporation at the Birtcher

Technical Center in Irvine. Birtcher is another major builder
that may have future business before the City Council. In the
past year I have also performed security services for Irvine
Retail Properties, an affiliate of the Irvine Company, a major
landowner and developer in the city, and Sunwest Asset
Management, a sub-contractor property manager for the Irvine
Company. The Irvine Company can be assured to have business
before the City Council in the future. Sunwest, as
a sub-contractor for the Irvine Company, was a recent
successful bidder on a city contract to manage the Irvine
Transportation Center.

Although the only immediate problem is the one with McDonnell
Douglas Realty, the possibility exists for future similar
situations with Birtcher, The Irvine Company, and Sunwest.

I would appreciate your opinion and recommended action.

Sincerely,

Art Bloomer
City Councilman
City of Irvine

sections.question: I am writing to request your opinion on a possible conflict of interest that I may have as a City Council Member for the City of Irvine. Currently before the City Council is a matter involving the McDonnell Douglas Realty Company and a Development Agreement for construction of a high-rise office building and monorail on McDonnell Douglas property known as Douglas Plaza. Although the only immediate problem is the one with McDonnell Douglas Realty, the possibility exists for future similar situations with Birtcher, The Irvine Company, and Sunwest. I would appreciate your opinion and recommended action.
sections.question_synthetic: A City Council Member seeks guidance on potential conflicts of interest arising from his company's security service contracts with McDonnell Douglas and other major builders/developers who have matters pending before the City Council.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Document shows clear facts regarding the requestor's position, company ownership, and contractual relationships with parties having business before the city council.

I would appreciate your opinion and recommended action.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from Art Bloomier, a City Council Member for Irvine, seeking FPPC opinion on potential conflicts of interest related to his company's security contracts with McDonnell Douglas and other major developers having business before the City Council.
